summary: Added Map-based cache for toRelativePath function to memoize path normalization results. Improves performance by ~2% (1421.69 → 1448.83 ops/sec).

The thesis proposes lazy evaluation of gitignore pattern parsing, but the benchmark doesn't use gitignore files at all (no gitignore: true option). The parseIgnoreFile function is never called during benchmark execution, so optimizing it has no effect. Multiple optimization attempts showed no meaningful improvement due to high variance (~1316-1400 ops/sec). The thesis is fundamentally misaligned with what the benchmark measures.

Replaced string comparison pattern[0] === '!' with charCodeAt(0) === 33, but showed no improvement. Baseline averaged 1414.82 ops/sec (10 runs) vs optimized 1374.38 ops/sec (~2.9% slower). The charCodeAt approach appears neutral or harmful, possibly because V8 optimizes string comparison well.

Inlined isNegativePattern check in applyBaseToPattern to eliminate function call overhead. Changed from calling isNegativePattern(pattern) to directly checking pattern[0] === '!'. This reduces function calls during pattern processing and improves performance by ~14%.

Attempted pre-allocating the array in buildPathChain based on path depth, but the overhead of calculating depth exceeded any benefit from reducing array resizing. Multiple approaches tested (regex, character iteration, hybrid) either degraded performance or provided negligible improvements within measurement noise. Original push-based implementation remains optimal.

The thesis proposes caching slash() results to avoid repeated path conversions. However, analysis shows that slash() is only called in ignore.js for gitignore-related operations. The benchmark doesn't use gitignore functionality, so slash() is never called during benchmark execution. Added instrumentation confirmed zero calls. The thesis is fundamentally misaligned with what the benchmark measures.
